Class DataItemGenerator

java.lang.Object
de.ukbonn.mwtek.dashboardlogic.DataItemGenerator
Direct Known Subclasses:
AcribisDataItemGenerator, BctDataItemGenerator, KidsRadarDataItemGenerator, KiRaKjpDataItemGenerator, KiRaPedDataItemGenerator
public class DataItemGenerator extends Object
Class in which the individual DataItems of the Json specification are generated.

  • Constructor Details

    • DataItemGenerator

      public DataItemGenerator(List<MiiCondition> conditions, List<MiiObservation> observations, List<MiiPatient> patients, List<MiiEncounter> encounters, List<MiiProcedure> procedures, List<MiiLocation> locations)
      Initialization of the CoronaResults object with the required FhirRessource lists.
      Parameters:
      conditions - List of FHIR condition resources for U07.1 and U07.2 diagnoses
      observations - List of FHIR Observation Resources based on the Corona Lab Codes
      patients - List of FHIR patient resources based on the Condition and Observation inputs
      encounters - List of FHIR encounter resources based on the Condition and Observation inputs
      procedures - List of FHIR ICU procedures resources based on the Patient/Encounter inputs
      locations - List of all FHIR location resources based on the encounter inputs

  • Method Details

    • getDataItems

      public List<DiseaseDataItem> getDataItems(Map<String,Boolean> mapExcludeDataItems, VariantSettings variantSettings, InputCodeSettings inputCodeSettings, QualitativeLabCodesSettings qualitativeLabCodesSettings, DataItemContext dataItemContext, GlobalConfiguration globalConfiguration)
      Creation of the JSON specification file for the Corona dashboard based on FHIR resources.
      Parameters:
      mapExcludeDataItems - Map with data items to be excluded from the output (e.g. "current.treatmentlevel").
      variantSettings - Configuration of extended covid-19 variants for the definition of not yet known/captured variants.
      inputCodeSettings - The configuration of the parameterizable codes such as the observation codes or procedure codes.
      globalConfiguration - An instance of the global configuration settings.
      Returns:
      List with all the data items that are defined in the corona dashboard json specification

    • isItemNotExcluded

      protected static boolean isItemNotExcluded(Map<String,Boolean> mapExcludeDataItems, String currentMaxtreatmentlevelLabel, boolean defaultValue)

      protected String addDebugLabel(String dataItem)

    • reportAttributeArtifacts

      protected void reportAttributeArtifacts(List<MiiEncounter> encounters)

    • filterEncounterByStatus

      protected List<MiiEncounter> filterEncounterByStatus(List<MiiEncounter> encounters, GlobalConfiguration globalConfiguration)
      Filters out encounters with invalid status, unless explicitly allowed via configuration.

      This method should theoretically never exclude any encounters in production, since encounter status validation is already enforced during data retrieval. It exists primarily as a safeguard and is especially relevant for unit tests. An encounter is considered valid if:

      • isEncounterStatusValid() returns true, or
      • getStatus() == UNKNOWN and the global flag useOutpatientEncounterWithStatusUnknown is enabled
      Parameters:
      encounters - the full list of encounters to validate
      globalConfiguration - global config containing feature flags
      Returns:
      list of encounters considered valid for further processing

    • getMapCurrentTreatmentlevelCasenrs

      public Map<String, List<String>> getMapCurrentTreatmentlevelCasenrs()

    • determineLabel

      public static String determineLabel(DataItemContext context, String defaultLabel)
      Some data items generate the same output, but are based on different data contexts. Different prefixes are set depending on this context.

    • determineKiRaLabel

      public static String determineKiRaLabel(KidsRadarDataItemContext context, String defaultLabel)
      Some data items generate the same output, but are based on different data contexts. Different prefixes are set depending on this context.
